hero.question: Will Apple be the second-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31?
hero.prob_take: At 50.5%, this market is pricing Apple and Saudi Aramco as true coin flips, which means the crowd has zero conviction on whether the Magnificent Seven continues to crush or Saudi's oil wealth finally translates to market cap dominance. Aramco needs a 28% rally to dethrone Apple, but oil at $80 and geopolitical risk priced in makes that bet overvalued at even money.

daily_take.headline: Apple flips a coin on second place. The market just woke up.
daily_take.deck: Apple traded up 3.5 points in 24 hours to dead even (50.5%), signaling serious uncertainty about whether it holds the #2 spot by March 31. The move reeks of macro jitters, not fundamental repricing. At even odds, you're getting paid exactly nothing for the uncertainty here, which means smart money should wait for a clearer signal or hunt for edges in the other three months of the quarter. Flip, don't chase.

sidebar.headline: France's political establishment rallying behind an insurgent. Dati's +4.5 point surge to 18.5% smells like consolidation, but Paris mayors don't move markets. Watch if she breaks 25%.
sidebar.label: Rachida Dati: 18.5%

sidebar.headline: Arizona getting love from March Madness believers. Up 2.2 points at 19.4%, but mid-tier teams stay mid-tier. Better value in next-tier schools catching late money.
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 Tournament: 19.4%

sidebar.headline: Defense startup IPO odds imploded 22 points in one day. Anduril just raised at $8B valuation, killing pre-2027 IPO thesis. This wasn't mispriced, this was outdated.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO before 2027: 25.5%
